# Suize — the payment rail for AI agents on Sui

> Suize lets any business charge payments or subscriptions to any AI agent, in
> USDC on Sui — and lets a human give their AI a capped, non-custodial balance
> to spend from. If you are an agent reading this, this file tells you what is
> real today, how to get paid, and how to pay.

Suize is deterministic payment rails, not an AI. The backend ships no agent,
no chat, no inference; it never emits a number that lands in a transaction and
it never signs an owner transaction. Status: pre-launch, Sui TESTNET phase —
the rail contract is written and unit-tested but NOT yet published on-chain.
No "live on mainnet" claim is made here; no real funds sit behind unaudited
code.

## The two faces (everything is one of these)

1. CHARGE — the open rail (the Stripe half). Any agent that can pay USDC on
   Sui can pay a Suize merchant — one-off or subscription — with nothing
   Suize-specific on the payer side. Suize takes 2%, inline at settlement,
   emitted in the on-chain receipt event (visible, never hidden). No KYB.
2. PAY — the consumer side (the Revolut half). A human funds an on-chain USDC
   Account for their AI: the deposit IS the hard spend cap, withdraw/kill is
   an instant on-chain act, and every action leaves a verifiable trace. The
   conversational AI wallet app on top of this is ROADMAP — the on-chain rail
   underneath is what exists.

## The rail — four on-chain verbs (`account.move`)

- `spend(account, amount, payee, memo)` — owner-signed direct send. FREE, no
  fee, full amount to the payee.
- `charge(account, config, merchant, amount, memo)` — one-off merchant charge
  from a funded Account. 2% inline.
- `charge_subscription(account, config, sub_id, amount, clock)` — recurring
  charge; permissionless to trigger but leashed by on-chain terms (fixed
  payee + per-period cap + Clock). 2% inline.
- `pay(merchant_account, config, coin, memo)` — one-off from ANY raw payer;
  no Suize Account needed. The open door for external agents. 2% inline.

Every merchant-paid verb emits a receipt event with the fee visible. The
contract is written and tested but UNPUBLISHED — there is no live package id
yet, so no Suize payment settles on-chain today. Treat this section as the
contract you will integrate against, not a live endpoint.

## How to get paid (merchants)

Return HTTP 402 from your endpoint — "pay X USDC to <merchant address> via
Suize" — carrying a Suize pay-link. 402-shaped, x402-compatible by design;
Suize does not run an x402 facilitator (none exists live on Sui). Proof of
payment is the on-chain receipt event from `pay()`/`charge()` — key "is it
paid?" off that receipt; a raw transfer emits none. No KYB, no onboarding
gate. The drop-in merchant snippet/SDK is in development (roadmap).

## How to pay (agents)

- You hold your own Sui wallet + USDC: build the `pay(merchant, coin, memo)`
  transaction against the rail package, sign with your own key, submit.
  Permissionless — no Suize account, no Suize SDK. (Available once the rail
  package publishes.)
- You cannot build a Sui transaction: the merchant's 402 carries a pay-link
  to a Suize-hosted confirm page — hand it to your user; one tap settles.
- Your user funded a Suize Account for you: charges are signed by the OWNER's
  local zkLogin session, gated by client-side confirm dials (confirm-each by
  default; auto-under-$X / full-auto only if the user switched them on).
  Subscriptions, once approved by the user, renew silently.
- Optional developer integration (in development): a LOCAL MCP whose
  `suize_pay` tool acts as the 402 client — reads the 402, checks the confirm
  dial, signs with the local session, settles. A dev tool, not a consumer
  product. There is NO remote/hosted MCP connector.

## Custody (exact, no marketing)

Fully non-custodial — the user's keys never leave their machine. Sign-in is
Google zkLogin; the user's local session is the only signer of owner
transactions; the Suize backend never signs one and never holds the key.
There is NO `set_agent`, NO delegated agent key, NO on-chain agent identity —
you, the agent, hold no key at all. Honest caveat: the funded Account is
delegated-spend (v1 has no payee allow-list), bounded by the deposit (hard
cap), the verifiable receipt trail, and a one-tap on-chain kill (`withdraw` /
`cancel_subscription`). Delegated-spend risk, not custody risk.

## How to care for your user (required behavior)

You spend your user's real money. Hold to these:

- Respect the confirm dials. Confirm-each is the default; only go faster if
  the user explicitly set auto-under-$X or full-auto.
- Never exceed the funded balance. The chain enforces the cap; do not attempt
  payments you know will fail or drain the Account.
- Flag unknown or unexpected payees before paying. v1 has no payee
  allow-list — your judgment is the soft guard on top of the hard cap.
- Surface the receipt. After paying, show the user the on-chain receipt (tx
  digest, amount, the visible fee). Lead with proof.
- Respect the kill. The user can withdraw the balance or cancel a
  subscription on-chain at any time. If a payment fails for that reason,
  stop — do not retry, do not route around it. Tell the user.
- Say "autonomy you switch on," never "fully autonomous." The user is always
  in control.

## Real today vs roadmap (honest ledger)

REAL today (testnet):

- Deploy by Suize — POST a built static site → stored on Walrus → an
  immutable on-chain Site object → live at `https://<id>.suize.site`, every
  byte hash-verified at serve time. Proven end-to-end: Suize's own landing
  ships through it. HTTP contract: `GET /auth/nonce` → signed
  `POST /deploy` at https://api.suize.io (agent snippets on the dashboard).
  Pricing model: $0.50 one-off per deploy, plus an optional $19.99/mo
  subscription for custom domains + auto-renewed Walrus storage — billing
  settles on the rail and activates when the rail publishes; today deploys
  are auth-gated, not billed.
- Crash by Suize — a live BTC up/down game on Sui TESTNET with gasless
  Google-zkLogin play. Testnet only; no real funds.

ROADMAP (not built or not live — do not assume):

- The conversational consumer AI wallet (the PAY app's chat layer).
- The on-chain publish of the rail (`account.move`), then mainnet.
- The merchant 402 snippet/SDK as a published package.
- The local MCP `suize_pay` / `suize_deploy` developer tools.
- The subscription relayer (the recurring-charge trigger).
- Merchant discovery/recommendation to PAY users.

- Deploy dashboard + agent HTTP contract: https://deploy.suize.io
- Deploy API host: https://api.suize.io
- Crash (testnet): https://crash.suize.io
